label: Data Governance & Ownership
description: The policies, roles, and accountability structures that determine how data decisions are made and enforced across the organisation.

label: Metadata & Cataloguing
description: The organisation's ability to document, discover, and understand its data assets — what exists, what it means, where it came from, and how it is used.

label: Data Quality & Observability
description: The practices and tooling used to measure, monitor, and improve the accuracy, completeness, timeliness, and consistency of data.

label: Storage & Compute Architecture
description: How data is stored, structured, and made available for query — spanning operational databases, analytical stores, lakehouses, and object storage.

label: Ingestion & Pipelines
description: The processes and tooling that move data from sources into analytical and operational systems, including batch ingestion, streaming, and change data capture.

label: Analytics & BI Delivery
description: How the organisation transforms data into insights — including reporting, dashboards, self-service analytics, and the semantic layer that makes data meaningful to business users.

label: Security, Privacy & Compliance
description: The controls, policies, and tooling that protect data from unauthorised access, ensure privacy obligations are met, and evidence compliance with regulatory requirements.

label: Team Skills & Operating Model
description: The human dimension: the skills, roles, and organisational structures that enable the data function to operate effectively and improve over time.
